content: BPSD is not a standalone DSM-5 diagnosis but rather a clinical syndrome occurring in context of major/neurocognitive disorder (dementia). Assessment involves: comprehensive history (symptom onset, frequency, severity, triggers), mental status examination, physical examination to rule out delirium/medical causes, medication review, laboratory tests (CBC, metabolic panel, thyroid, B12), brain imaging if indicated. Use standardized instruments: Neuropsychiatric Inventory (NPI), Cohen-Mansfield Agitation Inventory (CMAI), Cornell Scale for Depression in Dementia. Differentiate from delirium (acute onset, fluctuating course, inattention), primary psychiatric disorders, medication side effects. Document target symptoms for treatment monitoring.
